title: London (Chad/Coda Centre, Fulham) & Belgium: Ypres, Kortrijk, Oudenaarde, Ghent

main_route.original_wording: the Coda Centre, Munster Rd., Fulham, London
main_route.excerpt: We all arrived at the Coda Centre, Munster Rd., Fulham, London at 2.30 - 3.15. to put out the food ready for the Chad showroom opening.

geocode_hint.q: Coda Centre Munster Road Fulham London

main_route.excerpt: Then we went to the Channel Tunnel, eating garage sandwiches on the way.
Then we went to the Channel Tunnel, eating garage sandwiches on the way.
We shopped at a French supermarket & on to Belgium.
Kemmelcampsite *** near Ypra. V. quiet. Lovely barrel wheel for running in in the playground. The campsite is planted with trees.
We finally found the Vlax museum - specialising in linseed + flax. It is very difficult to find your way in Belgium because they only put signs actually at the turnings.
On to Oudenaarde where there is a lovely town hall (closed) in the square. Thomas looked around while we went to put the tent up in the Oudenaarde campsite ****.
On to Gent. The Altar was closed until 2pm. So we [window] shopped + had lunch at Belfort.

boundary_flags: mixed_start: page A_0191_R begins mid-entry with content from a prior trip (Malvern farm, Tonic the dog); the current trip starts at the 'Wed Oct 23rd' heading
mixed_end: page A_0194_R contains only a postcard caption ('Just Judges and Knights of Christ') — a Van Eyck panel — and may be the last page of this trip or a boundary page
summary: In October 1996 the family drove from Birmingham to the Coda Centre in Fulham, London, for the opening of a Chad lighting showroom, sleeping overnight in the van. The following day they took the Channel Tunnel to France and drove on to the Kemmel campsite near Ypres, Belgium, making a day trip to the Interior Exhibition in Kortrijk before moving on to Oudenaarde and finally visiting Ghent — including the Van Eyck Altarpiece at St Bavo's — before returning home.
extraction_notes: 1. Page A_0191_R is a mixed-boundary page; the top portion ('Sat 14' entry) belongs to a prior trip (probably a Malvern/farm outing); this trip begins at the 'Wed Oct 23rd' heading on the same page. 2. The diary uses 'Ypra' for Ypres/Ieper. 3. The 'Vlax museum' is almost certainly the Nationaal Vlasmuseum in Kortrijk (on the Leie), but is recorded in unresolved given the spelling. 4. The date_end of 1996-10-27 (Sunday) is inferred: Fri=25th, Sat=26th, departure from Gent at 5:40 implies Sunday 27th return. 5. France appears in countries because they transited through northern France (supermarket stop) even though no French overnight base was used. 6. The return Channel Tunnel crossing is inferred (not explicitly stated).
